text: A memory-bounded language model should keep facts that are useful later in the generation.
The Eiffel Tower is in Paris, and a question about its city should preserve that earlier token.
In a long story, the name of the main character can matter again near the ending.
KV cache compression tries to reduce memory while preserving the tokens needed for attention.
Retention scores decay over time, but important tokens can still remain in the cache.
A model answering a math problem may need to remember the original numbers after several steps.
Some tokens are structural, such as punctuation and formatting, while others carry factual content.
The TrimKV gate predicts whether a token should last under a fixed cache budget.
Training the gate with a capacity penalty encourages the expected cache size to stay small.
A short sanity dataset cannot prove benchmark quality, but it can reveal crashes and shape errors.
A user asks where the bridge is located. The earlier sentence said the Golden Gate Bridge is in San Francisco.
Long-context reasoning often depends on a small number of facts spread across many filler sentences.
The cache should not keep every repeated word when a compact set of useful facts is enough.
When the memory budget is tight, eviction should prefer stale and low-value tokens.
A retention gate is useful because it assigns importance when a token is created, not only after attention is computed.
The recipe starts with flour, salt, and water. Later instructions depend on remembering those ingredients.
In a dialogue, the speaker's preference can appear early and affect the final recommendation.
A document may mention that Alice owns the red notebook, and a later question may ask who owns it.
The capital of Japan is Tokyo, so the token Tokyo should remain available for location questions.
Compressing the KV cache is different from shortening the prompt because generation still needs selected old states.
A low retention score means the token can decay quickly as decoding moves forward.
A high retention score means the token can survive longer under the same memory budget.
The model should remember that the conference deadline is Friday even after reading several unrelated details.
For arithmetic, the numbers 17 and 23 should be retained until the final sum is produced.
For summarization, names, dates, places, and causal events are often more useful than repeated function words.
A fixed-size KV cache must decide which old tokens to evict once new tokens arrive.
TrimKV uses a learned gate so the model can discover token utility from data instead of a hand-written rule.
The capacity loss is soft: it penalizes expected occupancy above the budget during training.
A good cache policy should keep sink tokens, important entities, and useful recent context when possible.
A bad cache policy may evict the only token that answers a later factual question.
The book title was The Silent River, and the final answer should preserve that exact title.
The hospital appointment is at 9 AM, and later scheduling text depends on that time.
A generated proof should keep assumptions available while deriving later conclusions.
The weather report said rain begins after sunset, which matters for the final travel advice.
In code completion, variable names introduced earlier can be more important than comments far away.
Cache eviction should be deterministic for a fixed model state and prompt during greedy decoding.
A model can use retention bias during training and hard eviction during inference.
The gate output beta lies between zero and one, making exponential decay simple and stable.
Using log beta avoids numerical underflow when token ages become large.
Grouped-query attention shares KV heads across multiple query heads, so the cache must support that shape.
A training example should contain enough context for the gate to see which tokens become useful later.
The answer to the riddle was candle, so the token candle should not disappear before the conclusion.
The customer asked for a vegan meal, and later recommendations must respect that constraint.
The route goes from Delhi to Jaipur, and the destination token Jaipur should remain accessible.
In legal text, definitions introduced early can affect clauses that appear many paragraphs later.
In scientific writing, units such as meters, seconds, and kilograms can be critical to later calculations.
A compressed cache should reduce memory use without changing the model's output too much.
The teacher model provides a target distribution so the student with TrimKV can preserve quality.
The gate checkpoint stores only the small retention modules, not a full copy of the base language model.
This fifty-example dataset is still small, but it gives a better training smoke test than ten examples.
